personas.name: Product
personas.summary: The product person who decides WHAT gets built — runs research, produces wireframes, and writes feature specs, bundled as 'spec kits' that the Outcome Engineer then implements via flows. Lives upstream of any code.

traditional.role: Product — owns the 'what'. Generates research, produces wireframes, and authors feature specs, packaged together as spec kits. Hands implementable spec kits downstream to the Outcome Engineer. Does not write production code or configure infrastructure.
goals: Produce clear, well-grounded research, wireframes, and feature specs (spec kits) that an implementer can act on without guessing.
Keep the chain traceable — research → wireframe → feature spec → the user stories it implies.
Iterate on a spec quickly when validation surfaces that it didn't land, without losing the rationale.
See which features are specced, which are in-flight, and where the spec backlog has gaps.
frustrations: Specs get misinterpreted in implementation; intent is lost in translation.
No traceability from research to spec to the working feature.
Wireframes drift from specs (and from what actually ships); the spec kit fragments.
Hard to package research + wireframes + feature spec into one coherent handed-off artifact instead of scattered docs.
traditional.context: Works upstream of code. Produces artifacts (research, .pen wireframes, feature-spec docs) that become inputs to implementation flows. Collaborates with the Outcome Engineer and relies on the Principal Product Engineer's setup. Regulated environment (FERPA/COPPA via SkoolScout) — specs touching user/student data carry compliance constraints that must surface in the spec.
traditional.techProfile: Strong product/design sensibility; comfortable with structured artifacts, wireframing tools (Pencil/.pen), and reading code/diffs at a review level but not authoring them. Wants the console to make a spec kit feel like one versioned, traceable object.

researchSources.summary: Initial hypothesis from the builder's domain knowledge; no formal user research yet.

personas.name: Outcome Engineer
personas.summary: Takes product spec kits and turns them into working features — submits them as job requests against flows, watches the implementation run, and validates the features that come back. Owns the submit-and-validate loop.

traditional.role: Outcome Engineer — owns 'did it actually get built, correctly'. Takes spec kits from Product, submits them as job requests to the appropriate flow, monitors the implementation job, validates the resulting feature against the spec — accept, reject, or send back for another iteration.
goals: Turn a spec kit into a working, validated feature as fast as the loop allows.
Know quickly whether the agent understood the spec — and where it didn't — without reading every line by hand.
Validate generated features against explicit, spec-derived criteria (functional, security, regulated-data) and record a clear pass/fail with rationale.
Iterate efficiently when a feature misses — re-submit with corrections, not start over — and keep an audit trail of attempts.
frustrations: Hard to tell if the agent understood the spec; failures are discovered late, by hand.
Validation is manual and tedious — no spec-derived checklist, no diff-in-context view, lots of GitHub tab-switching.
No clear pass/fail criteria carried from the spec into the review.
Iterating means re-submitting and waiting, with poor visibility into what changed between attempts.
traditional.context: Sits between Product (source of spec kits) and the flows authored by the Principal Product Engineer. Reviews on their own cadence, not real-time. Regulated environment (FERPA/COPPA via SkoolScout): every accept/reject must be attributable and logged.
traditional.techProfile: Engineer who reads code/diffs fluently and runs tests. Wants diff-centric, annotation-friendly review surfaces with provenance (which spec, which flow, which job, which agent) and spec-derived validation checklists. Comfortable in a dark, dense console.

researchSources.summary: Initial hypothesis from the builder's domain knowledge; no formal user research yet.

personas.name: Principal Product Engineer
personas.summary: Owns the general setup that makes AgentX run for a product — registers the product and its repos, configures the default context source, and authors the FlowDefs (incl. publish-* PR/merge nodes) that job requests resolve to. The infrastructure layer beneath Product and Outcome Engineer.

traditional.role: Principal Product Engineer — owns 'general setup'. For each productId: registers it and its existing repos, sets the default context source (what agentx-load feeds the agents), and authors/maintains the FlowDefs job requests run through — including the publish-* nodes that open PRs and merge under the controlplane-issued GitHub App token.
goals: Register a product and its repos quickly and correctly — repos, credentials, context config as one coherent setup, in an obvious order.
Define a default context source that gives agents the right grounding.
Author and iterate on Flows that cover the job-request types the product needs, with confidence they're correct before a real job hits them.
Trust that an incoming job request resolves to the intended flow with the intended context — and see where flow coverage has gaps.
frustrations: Unclear what 'registering a product' actually requires — repos? credentials? context source? — and in what order.
Context-source setup is opaque: no easy way to confirm the agent is seeing the right code/docs.
Composing/editing FlowDefs is fiddly; no good 'does this flow even work?' check short of running a real job.
Hard to see which flows exist for a product, which job-request types they cover, and where the gaps are.
traditional.context: The setup/infrastructure persona. Sets a product up once, then evolves its flows and context config. Senior enough to be trusted with publish/merge flow authoring (Gate-2: controlplane is the credential authority). Regulated environment — flow/context changes should be attributable.
traditional.techProfile: Senior engineer. Fluent with repos, YAML/config, CI concepts, containers/DevContainers. Wants the console to be a faster, safer path to product + flow configuration than hand-editing files.

researchSources.summary: Initial hypothesis from the builder's domain knowledge; no formal user research yet.

journeys.name: Register a product
journeys.summary: Principal Product Engineer brings a product into AgentX: name + productId, attach existing repos, configure the default context source, activate. The setup prerequisite for any job request.

stages.name: Start product registration
userActions: Open Intake → 'New product'
Enter product name; choose or accept a productId (slug)
stages.systemResponse: Creates a draft product record; prompts for repos next; shows the setup checklist (repos → context → activate).

stages.name: Attach existing repos
userActions: Connect one or more existing repos (org/repo or URL)
Pick a default branch per repo
Authorize the GitHub App on the org if not already installed
stages.systemResponse: Validates repo access via the GitHub App; records repos + default branches; surfaces access errors inline.

stages.name: Configure the default context source
userActions: Define what agentx-load pulls as grounding (paths/globs, docs, prior context)
Pick a context strategy / scope
Preview the resolved context (files + token estimate)
stages.systemResponse: Resolves the context source; renders a preview of what would be loaded (file list + token count); warns if over budget.

stages.name: Confirm & activate the product
userActions: Review the assembled product (name, productId, repos, context source)
Activate it
stages.systemResponse: Marks the product active; job requests can now target it; prompts 'no flows yet → author a flow' with a link to the author-flow path.

journeys.name: Author a Flow (FlowDef)
journeys.summary: Principal Product Engineer composes a FlowDef to cover a job-request type — pick the type, wire nodes (clone → load-context → run-agent → tests → publish-pr → publish-merge), validate, save & version. Enumerates the flows needed to support job requests via a coverage matrix.

stages.name: Pick a flow to author / job-request type to cover
userActions: Open Compose → 'New flow'; name it
Choose which job-request type(s) it serves (implement-feature, fix-bug, run-benchmark, generate-spec/research, review-proposal, maintenance/dep-upgrade)
Review the coverage matrix: which job-request types already have a flow, which don't
stages.systemResponse: Scaffolds an empty flow graph; renders the product's flow-coverage matrix highlighting uncovered job-request types.

stages.name: Add & wire nodes
userActions: Add nodes: clone-repo → load-context → run-agent → run-tests → publish-pr → publish-merge (+ branches/conditions)
Wire skills/agents from the Catalog into run-agent
Set per-node config
stages.systemResponse: Live-validates the graph (disconnected nodes, missing required config, publish-merge without a preceding publish-pr, etc.); flags issues inline on the canvas.

stages.name: Validate the flow (dry-run)
userActions: Run a dry-run / validation pass against a sample input (no real agent execution, no real PR)
Read the per-node pass/fail report
stages.systemResponse: Walks the graph; checks each node would resolve (repo accessible, context loads, skills enabled, GitHub App token available for publish-*); reports pass/fail per node with reasons.

stages.name: Save & version the flow
userActions: Save the FlowDef with a version note
Optionally mark it as the default flow for a job-request type
stages.systemResponse: Persists the FlowDef; bumps version; updates the coverage matrix; job requests of that type now resolve to it. Shows version history with rollback.

journeys.name: Test a flow
journeys.summary: Principal Product Engineer submits a real (sandboxed) job against a newly-authored flow, watches it run node-by-node, inspects the result, and either marks the flow ready or loops back to Compose to fix it.

stages.name: Submit a test job against the flow
userActions: From Compose (or SubmitJob), submit a job request targeting the product + the new flow with a small/sample input
Choose a sandbox / no-publish mode so publish-* nodes don't create real PRs
stages.systemResponse: Enqueues the job; spins up the agentx-job-<jobId> DevContainer; starts agents per the flow; marks it a test/sandboxed run.

stages.name: Watch the run
userActions: Open Jobs → the test job → Sessions; watch nodes execute, agent logs stream, worktree changes
stages.systemResponse: Live status per node; current node highlighted on the flow graph; streaming logs; per-worktree changes.

stages.name: Inspect the result
userActions: When the job finishes/fails, inspect the output — proposal (diff), PR (if publish-pr ran), test results, logs
If a node failed, drill into the failure
stages.systemResponse: Shows produced artifacts, per-node outcomes, failure reasons + retry affordance; a 'which node failed and why' summary.

stages.name: Decide — flow's good, or iterate
userActions: Either mark the flow validated/ready, or go back to Compose to fix it (loop to 'Author a Flow', step 2)
stages.systemResponse: Records the test outcome against the flow version (input X → result Y, pass/fail).

journeys.name: Spec a feature (build a spec kit)
journeys.summary: Product persona builds a spec kit: frame the feature → generate/attach research → produce wireframes → write the feature spec + acceptance criteria + user stories → package & version. The kit is one versioned object (mirrors product/.pencil-ux.json + .pen + feature-spec doc).

stages.name: Frame the feature / start a spec kit
userActions: Open the Specs/Feature-Kits surface → 'New feature kit'
Name it; write the problem statement / intent; link the persona(s) it serves
stages.systemResponse: Creates a draft spec kit; shows its sections (research → wireframes → feature spec → stories) as a checklist.

stages.name: Generate / attach research
userActions: Run a generate-research job against a brief, OR attach existing research
Review the research output and link the findings that matter
stages.systemResponse: If generating: submits the job, shows progress, attaches the result to the kit. If attaching: links it. Lets findings be cited from spec sections later.

stages.name: Produce wireframes
userActions: Open Pencil (or a generate-wireframes job) to produce low-fi screens for the feature
Review; attach the .pen artifacts to the kit; link each wireframe to the spec section it realizes
stages.systemResponse: Links the .pen artifacts to the kit; shows them inline; tracks the wireframe ↔ spec-section mapping.

stages.name: Write the feature spec + acceptance criteria + stories
userActions: Write the feature spec (scope, behavior, constraints incl. regulated-data, out-of-scope)
Derive the user stories; set acceptance criteria per story (Given/When/Then)
stages.systemResponse: Validates the spec has the required sections; flags missing regulated-data handling if the feature/persona touches user/student data; lists the implied stories.

stages.name: Package & version the spec kit
userActions: Review the assembled kit (research + wireframes + spec + stories)
Package it; give it a version note; mark it ready to hand off
stages.systemResponse: Bundles into a versioned spec-kit artifact (mirrors product/.pencil-ux.json + .pen + feature-spec doc); marks it available to the Outcome Engineer; shows version history.

journeys.name: Hand off a spec kit to the Outcome Engineer
journeys.summary: Product hands a packaged spec kit to the Outcome Engineer: select the kit + target product/flow → hand off (kit enters the OE's queue with provenance) → track its lifecycle status.

stages.name: Select a packaged kit & target
userActions: Pick a ready spec kit
Identify the product/repo it's for; optionally the suggested flow (implement-feature)
stages.systemResponse: Shows the kit summary + the target product's available flows; confirms the kit is complete and the flow can accept its shape.

stages.name: Hand off
userActions: Hand the kit to the Outcome Engineer (assign / notify / queue)
stages.systemResponse: Records the handoff; the kit appears in the Outcome Engineer's queue with provenance (which spec, version, persona, target product/flow).

stages.name: Track status
userActions: Watch the kit's status as the OE submits it, a job runs, a feature comes back
stages.systemResponse: Surfaces the kit's lifecycle state: handed-off → submitted → running → produced → accepted/rejected.

journeys.name: Review implementation against the spec
journeys.summary: When a feature comes back, Product reviews it against the originating spec kit: open the produced feature (diff + kit side by side) → assess against the spec's acceptance criteria → decide spec-was-right (back to flow) vs spec-was-wrong (revise the kit, bump version, re-hand-off).

stages.name: Open the produced feature
userActions: From the Specs surface (or Proposals), open the feature a flow produced for this kit
See the diff + the originating kit side by side
stages.systemResponse: Links the proposal/PR to the originating spec kit; shows the spec's acceptance criteria alongside the diff.

stages.name: Assess against acceptance criteria
userActions: Walk the spec's acceptance criteria against the implementation
Mark each met/missed/ambiguous with a note; flag spec ambiguities the implementation surfaced
stages.systemResponse: Renders a spec-derived checklist; records each criterion's status + note.

stages.name: Decide — spec was right (back to flow) / spec was wrong (revise spec)
userActions: Fork: implementation missed a correct spec → send back to the flow (OE re-submits). Spec was unclear/wrong → revise the spec kit (loop to 'Spec a feature', bump version) and re-hand-off
stages.systemResponse: Records the decision + rationale; routes accordingly; bumps the relevant version (flow re-run vs. kit revision).

journeys.name: Submit a spec kit to a flow
journeys.summary: Outcome Engineer takes a handed-off spec kit and submits it as a job request: pick the kit from the queue → choose the flow & configure the run (sandbox vs real, overrides, iteration note) → submit (job links back to the kit version).

stages.name: Pick a spec kit from the queue
userActions: Open SubmitJob → 'Queue'; see handed-off spec kits with provenance (spec, version, persona, target product)
Pick one
stages.systemResponse: Shows the kit's contents (spec, wireframes, acceptance criteria) + the target product's available flows.

stages.name: Choose the flow & configure the run
userActions: Pick the flow to submit against (defaults to the kit's suggested flow, e.g. implement-feature)
Set run config: repo/branch, sandbox vs real, model/agent overrides if allowed, iteration note if re-submitting
stages.systemResponse: Validates the kit's shape fits the flow; pre-fills from the kit's suggested flow + the product's defaults; warns on mismatches.

stages.name: Submit & confirm
userActions: Submit the job request
stages.systemResponse: Enqueues; resolves the kit + product context + flow into a concrete job; assigns a jobId; spins up agentx-job-<jobId>; links the job to the spec-kit version (provenance). Confirms with a link to watch.

journeys.name: Watch the implementation run
journeys.summary: Outcome Engineer monitors a running job: open it (flow graph, current node) → inspect live agent work (Sessions: streaming logs, worktree diffs, agent state) → intervene if needed (stop / retry node / re-submit) → see the outcome summary when it completes. Shares its shape with the Principal Product Engineer's test-flow.

stages.name: Open the running job
userActions: From Jobs (or the submit confirmation), open the job → its flow graph with the current node highlighted
stages.systemResponse: Live node status; the flow graph; elapsed time; current cost/token spend.

stages.name: Inspect live agent work
userActions: Open Sessions for the job; read streaming agent logs; view worktree changes as they happen; optionally watch multiple agents/worktrees side by side
stages.systemResponse: Streaming logs per agent; per-worktree diff; agent state (thinking / tool-calling / done).

stages.name: Intervene if needed
userActions: If a job is stuck/wrong: stop the job, retry a failed node, or adjust & re-submit
stages.systemResponse: Applies the action; records it (who, when, why); for retry, re-runs from the failed node.

stages.name: Job completes — see the outcome summary
userActions: When the job finishes/fails, see the outcome summary: produced artifacts (proposal/diff, PR if any), per-node outcomes, total cost/time, 'which node failed and why' if failed
stages.systemResponse: The summary view; links to the produced feature for validation.

journeys.name: Validate & decide on the produced feature
journeys.summary: Outcome Engineer validates a produced feature against its spec: open it side by side with the spec kit → review automated checks → walk the acceptance-criteria checklist (with diff-line links) → decide accept / reject→flow / reject→Product / iterate. Decisions are attributable and logged (regulated).

stages.name: Open the produced feature against its spec
userActions: From Proposals (or the job outcome), open the produced feature — diff, the originating spec kit (criteria, wireframes), test results, the agent's own summary of what it did
stages.systemResponse: Links proposal ↔ spec-kit version ↔ job; shows the spec-derived acceptance-criteria checklist alongside the diff.

stages.name: Run / review automated checks
userActions: Look at the review-proposal flow's output (lint, tests, security scan, spec-coverage); run additional checks if needed
stages.systemResponse: Shows automated-check results inline on the diff; flags spec criteria with no test coverage.

stages.name: Walk the acceptance-criteria checklist
userActions: Mark each spec criterion met/missed/ambiguous with a note + the diff line(s) it relates to
Add code/security findings; flag spec ambiguities
stages.systemResponse: Records each criterion's status; tallies pass/fail; preserves the checklist so Product can add spec-conformance findings to the same one.

stages.name: Decide — accept / reject→flow / reject→Product / iterate
userActions: Accept → approve; publish-merge (or manual merge) lands it; kit lifecycle → accepted; audit record written
Reject→flow → implementation missed a correct spec; send back with corrections; re-submit (loop to oe-submit-kit step 2 with an iteration note)
Reject→Product → spec was wrong/unclear; route to Product's review-vs-spec / spec revision; kit lifecycle → needs-spec-revision
stages.systemResponse: Records the decision + rationale (attributable, logged — regulated); routes accordingly; updates the kit lifecycle state; writes the audit entry.

painPoints.description: Unclear what registering a product requires (repos? credentials? context source?) and in what order.
GitHub App install/authorization for repo access is opaque — can't tell if the App has access to a given private repo.
No way to test repo access before saving the product.
Can't confirm the agent will actually load the right code/docs — no preview of the resolved context.
Unclear how much context is too much (token budget) when configuring the context source.
A product can be activated with zero flows, so job requests against it just fail.
Hard to see which job-request types have a flow and which don't.
Composing/editing a FlowDef is fiddly; there's no 'does this even work?' check short of a real run.
Flow validation that doesn't actually execute can give false confidence about what 'valid' guarantees.
Unclear whether saving overwrote the previous flow version, or how to roll back.
A test run can have real side effects (e.g. open a real PR) — no sandbox/no-publish mode.
Can't tell which node a job is on, or whether it's stuck vs. just slow.
Failure reasons are buried in logs; no 'which node failed and why' summary.
No durable record of 'this flow version was tested with input X, produced Y'.
Research, wireframes, and the feature spec are scattered docs, not one packaged, versioned object.
Can't tell which research finding drove which spec decision — no citation link from spec sections to research.
Wireframes drift from the spec (and from what ships); no link between a wireframe and the spec section it realizes.
Regulated-data handling constraints (FERPA/COPPA) get forgotten until late in the spec.
No enforced spec structure → specs vary in completeness; implementers get incomplete specs.
Hard to tell if a packaged spec kit is complete and self-contained before handing it off.
Unclear which flow a spec kit should go to; the kit's shape and the flow's expectations can mismatch.
No clear ownership transfer at handoff — who's responsible for the kit now?
Product loses visibility into a spec kit once it leaves their hands (submitted → running → produced).
Can't see the produced feature against its spec (acceptance criteria, wireframes) in one place.
Acceptance-criteria assessment is improvised, not a structured spec-derived checklist.
The 'spec was wrong' vs 'implementation was wrong' fork on a rejected feature is muddy — features get misrouted (back to flow vs. back to Product).
The submit queue lacks context — priority, what changed since last look, why this kit now.
No estimate of how long a job will take or how much it will cost before submitting it.
Agent logs are a firehose — hard to see what the agent decided vs. raw output.
N agents × M worktrees overwhelms a single watch view.
Unclear what intervening (stop / retry node / re-submit) will do — re-run everything? destructive?
Automated checks (the review-proposal flow) and human review are disconnected — re-checking the same things by hand.
No diff-line ↔ acceptance-criterion linking → review is two disconnected reads (the diff, then the checklist).
When sending a feature back to a flow, there's no structured 'what corrections' payload → the next run repeats the mistake.

stories.title: Register a product in one guided flow

narrative.capability: register a product with its repos and a default context source in one guided flow
narrative.outcome: the product is ready to receive job requests without me guessing what's needed or in what order

acceptanceCriteria.given: I'm on Intake with no product yet
acceptanceCriteria.then: I see a setup checklist (name/productId → repos → context source → activate) and can't skip ahead past unmet steps

stories.title: Attach repos and confirm GitHub App access

narrative.capability: attach existing repos and confirm the GitHub App actually has access to each
narrative.outcome: flows can clone and push to them, and I find out about access problems now, not when a job fails

acceptanceCriteria.given: I add a private repo the GitHub App isn't installed on
acceptanceCriteria.then: I'm shown an inline error and a link to authorize/install the App on that org, and the repo isn't saved as accessible until access is confirmed

acceptanceCriteria.when: I view the repo in the product
acceptanceCriteria.then: I see a green 'access confirmed' state and the default branch

stories.title: Preview what the context source will load

narrative.capability: preview the file list and token estimate my configured context source would load
narrative.outcome: I can confirm the agent will see the right grounding before any job runs

acceptanceCriteria.given: I've configured a context source (paths/globs, docs)
acceptanceCriteria.then: I see the resolved file list and a token estimate, and a warning if it exceeds the budget

stories.title: Warn (don't block) on activating a product with no flows

narrative.capability: be warned, not blocked, when I activate a product that has no flows
narrative.outcome: I don't ship a product whose job requests will all fail, but I can still set it up incrementally

acceptanceCriteria.then: I get a warning ('no flows yet — job requests will fail until you author one') with a link to author a flow, and can proceed anyway

stories.title: See which job-request types have a flow

narrative.capability: see a coverage matrix of which job-request types my product has flows for and which it doesn't
narrative.outcome: I can spot and close flow-coverage gaps before a job request hits an uncovered type

acceptanceCriteria.given: a product with flows for some job-request types
acceptanceCriteria.when: I open Compose for the product
acceptanceCriteria.then: I see a matrix listing all job-request types, which have a flow (and which is default), and which are uncovered

stories.title: Compose a FlowDef by wiring nodes

narrative.capability: compose a FlowDef by adding and wiring nodes (clone → load-context → run-agent → run-tests → publish-pr → publish-merge, plus branches) with skills/agents from the Catalog
narrative.outcome: I can define exactly how a job-request type gets handled

acceptanceCriteria.when: I add nodes and connect them on the canvas
acceptanceCriteria.then: the flow graph reflects the wiring and each node exposes its required config

acceptanceCriteria.when: I pick skills/agents from the Catalog
acceptanceCriteria.then: they're attached to the node and the node shows them

stories.title: Live-validate the flow graph while editing

narrative.capability: have the composer live-validate the flow graph (disconnected nodes, missing required config, publish-merge without a preceding publish-pr)
narrative.outcome: I catch structural problems while editing instead of at runtime

acceptanceCriteria.given: a flow with a publish-merge node and no publish-pr upstream
acceptanceCriteria.then: the publish-merge node is flagged inline with the reason, and the flow can't be marked ready until resolved

stories.title: Dry-run a flow against a sample input

narrative.capability: dry-run a flow against a sample input (no real agent execution, no real PR) and get a per-node pass/fail report
narrative.outcome: I have confidence the flow's wiring resolves before a real job hits it

acceptanceCriteria.given: a composed flow and a sample input
acceptanceCriteria.then: I get a per-node report (repo accessible, context loads, skills enabled, GitHub App token available for publish-*) with pass/fail and reasons

stories.title: Version FlowDefs with history and rollback

narrative.capability: have every save of a FlowDef bump a version with a note, a history, and rollback
narrative.outcome: I can change a flow without fear of losing the previous working version

acceptanceCriteria.when: I save changes with a note
acceptanceCriteria.then: a new version is created, the history shows all versions, and I can roll back to any prior one

stories.title: Sandbox / no-publish run mode

narrative.capability: submit a job in a sandbox / no-publish mode where publish-* nodes don't create real PRs or merges
narrative.outcome: I can safely test a flow that ends in publish-merge, and iterate without polluting real repos

acceptanceCriteria.given: a flow with publish-pr / publish-merge nodes
acceptanceCriteria.when: I submit a job in sandbox mode
acceptanceCriteria.then: the publish-* nodes run in dry mode (logged, not executed), and the job/result is clearly marked 'sandbox'

stories.title: Test a flow and loop back to the composer

narrative.capability: submit a test job against a flow, watch it, see the result, and jump back to the composer if it's wrong — with the test outcome recorded against the flow version
narrative.outcome: I can iterate a flow to a known-good 'ready' state

acceptanceCriteria.then: the outcome (input → result, pass/fail) is recorded against the flow version, and I get a one-click path back to the composer for that version

stories.title: Job flow graph with the current node highlighted

narrative.role: Outcome Engineer or Principal Product Engineer
narrative.capability: see a running job's flow graph with the current node highlighted, per-node status, and elapsed time
narrative.outcome: I can tell which node the job is on and whether it's stuck or just slow

acceptanceCriteria.then: the flow graph shows each node's status (pending/running/done/failed), the current node is highlighted, and per-node elapsed time is visible

stories.title: Sessions: a decision trace above the raw log stream

narrative.role: Outcome Engineer or Principal Product Engineer
narrative.capability: see what the agent decided (a decision trace) surfaced above the raw log stream in Sessions
narrative.outcome: I can follow the agent's reasoning without drowning in tool-call noise

acceptanceCriteria.given: a job with streaming agent logs
acceptanceCriteria.then: I see a collapsible decision trace (agent chose X because Y) at the top, with the raw stream below, and can jump from a trace entry to its log lines

stories.title: Manageable N-agent × M-worktree watch view

narrative.role: Outcome Engineer or Principal Product Engineer
narrative.capability: view N agents × M worktrees in a collapsible, focusable layout
narrative.outcome: I can monitor a multi-agent job without one screen overwhelming me

acceptanceCriteria.given: a job with multiple agents and worktrees
acceptanceCriteria.then: agents/worktrees are listed collapsed by default, I can focus one to expand it, and the active one is indicated

stories.title: Job actions state their effects before confirming

narrative.role: Outcome Engineer or Principal Product Engineer
narrative.capability: have job actions (stop / retry node / re-submit) clearly state what they'll do before I confirm
narrative.outcome: I don't accidentally re-run everything or do something destructive

acceptanceCriteria.then: the confirm dialog states 'will re-run from <node>, keeping prior nodes' results' (or whatever the actual effect is) before I proceed

stories.title: 'Which node failed and why' summary on a failed job

narrative.role: Outcome Engineer or Principal Product Engineer
narrative.capability: see a 'which node failed and why' summary on a failed job
narrative.outcome: I don't have to spelunk logs to find the failure

acceptanceCriteria.then: the top of the page shows the failed node, a one-line cause, and a link to the relevant log lines and a retry action

stories.title: Live and final cost/time/token spend on a job

narrative.role: Outcome Engineer or Principal Product Engineer
narrative.capability: see live and final cost / time / token spend on a job
narrative.outcome: I can manage spend and notice runaway jobs

acceptanceCriteria.then: I see running cost/tokens/elapsed updating live; on completion the final totals are shown

stories.title: A spec kit is one versioned object

narrative.capability: build and manage a spec kit as one versioned object bundling research + wireframes + feature spec + user stories
narrative.outcome: I hand off a coherent artifact instead of scattered docs, and the implementer has everything in one place

acceptanceCriteria.given: I create a new feature kit
acceptanceCriteria.when: I add research, wireframes, a spec, and stories to it
acceptanceCriteria.then: they're all part of one kit object with a single version, and the kit shows a completeness checklist of its sections

stories.title: Cite research findings from spec sections

narrative.capability: cite research findings from the spec sections that rely on them
narrative.outcome: anyone can trace which research drove which decision

acceptanceCriteria.then: I can link one or more research findings to it, and the kit shows the research→decision links

stories.title: Link each wireframe to the spec section it realizes

narrative.capability: link each wireframe in a kit to the spec section it realizes
narrative.outcome: wireframes and spec don't drift apart, and reviewers can check the implementation against the right wireframe

acceptanceCriteria.given: a kit with attached .pen wireframes and spec sections
acceptanceCriteria.when: I associate a wireframe with a section
acceptanceCriteria.then: the kit shows the wireframe↔section map, and a section without a wireframe (or vice versa) is flagged

stories.title: Spec editor requires the structural sections

narrative.capability: have the spec editor require the structural sections (scope, behavior, regulated-data handling, out-of-scope, acceptance criteria)
narrative.outcome: every spec I hand off is complete, and regulated-data constraints aren't forgotten

acceptanceCriteria.given: a feature whose persona/scope touches user/student data
acceptanceCriteria.when: I try to package the kit without a regulated-data-handling section
acceptanceCriteria.then: packaging is blocked with a clear prompt to fill it in

stories.title: Package and version a spec kit with a completeness check

narrative.capability: package and version a spec kit and have the system check it's self-contained
narrative.outcome: I hand off something an implementer can act on without coming back for missing pieces

acceptanceCriteria.given: a kit with all required sections
acceptanceCriteria.then: a new kit version is created, marked ready-to-hand-off, with a version note, and any unresolved completeness issues block packaging

stories.title: Run generate-* flows from inside the spec kit

narrative.capability: run generate-research / generate-wireframes flows from inside a spec kit and watch them
narrative.outcome: I can produce parts of the kit with agents without leaving the surface

acceptanceCriteria.when: I run a generate-research job from within it
acceptanceCriteria.then: I watch the job inline and, on completion, the result is attached to the kit's research section

stories.title: Hand off a kit with provenance into the OE queue

narrative.capability: hand off a packaged kit to the Outcome Engineer with a target product/flow, landing it in their queue with full provenance
narrative.outcome: the implementer knows exactly what to build, where, and from which spec version

acceptanceCriteria.when: I hand it off to a target product (and optionally a suggested flow)
acceptanceCriteria.then: it appears in the Outcome Engineer's submit queue with provenance (spec id+version, persona, target product/flow), and the system warns if the kit shape doesn't fit the suggested flow

stories.title: Track a kit's lifecycle after handoff

narrative.capability: see a kit's lifecycle state (handed-off → submitted → running → produced → accepted/rejected) after handoff
narrative.outcome: I don't lose visibility once the kit leaves my hands

acceptanceCriteria.when: the Outcome Engineer submits it / a job runs / a feature is produced / a decision is made
acceptanceCriteria.then: the kit's lifecycle state updates on the Specs surface, with links to the relevant job and produced feature

stories.title: Produced feature shown side by side with its spec

narrative.capability: see the produced feature side by side with its spec (acceptance criteria, wireframes) and the diff
narrative.outcome: I can review conformance in one place instead of tab-switching between the diff and the spec

acceptanceCriteria.given: a job has produced a feature for a kit
acceptanceCriteria.then: the diff, the originating spec kit (criteria + wireframes), test results, and the agent's summary are shown together, linked (proposal ↔ kit version ↔ job)

stories.title: Shared, diff-linked acceptance-criteria checklist

narrative.capability: walk a spec-derived acceptance-criteria checklist, marking each criterion met/missed/ambiguous with a note and a link to the diff line(s) it relates to, shared across reviewers
narrative.outcome: review is structured and traceable, not improvised, and the OE's code findings and Product's spec findings live on one artifact

acceptanceCriteria.when: I mark a criterion 'missed' and link it to diff lines
acceptanceCriteria.then: the checklist records the status, note, and diff link, the overall pass/fail tally updates, and a co-reviewer sees my marks and can add theirs

stories.title: Automated checks pre-populate the review checklist

narrative.capability: have the review-proposal flow's automated checks (lint, tests, security, spec-coverage) pre-populate checklist items and show inline on the diff
narrative.outcome: I don't re-check by hand what the automated pass already verified

acceptanceCriteria.given: a feature with a completed review-proposal automated pass
acceptanceCriteria.then: automated findings appear as pre-filled checklist items (passing criteria pre-checked, failures flagged) and inline annotations on the diff

stories.title: Decide accept / reject→flow / reject→Product with audit

narrative.capability: decide accept / reject→flow / reject→Product on a produced feature with an explicit rationale, and have the system route accordingly and write an audit entry
narrative.outcome: rejected features go to the right place (re-run vs. spec revision) and every decision is attributable

acceptanceCriteria.when: I choose 'reject → Product' with a rationale
acceptanceCriteria.then: the kit lifecycle moves to needs-spec-revision, it's routed to Product's review-vs-spec, and an audit entry records who/when/why
it's queued for re-submission to the same flow with the rejection attached, and an audit entry is written

stories.title: reject→flow carries a structured corrections payload

narrative.capability: have a reject→flow carry a structured corrections payload (which criteria failed, which diff lines, what to do instead) that becomes context for the next run
narrative.outcome: the next iteration converges instead of repeating the same mistake

acceptanceCriteria.given: a feature I'm rejecting back to its flow
acceptanceCriteria.then: the failed criteria, linked diff lines, and my corrective notes are bundled as a corrections payload attached to the re-submission, and the next run's load-context node includes it

stories.title: Accepting a feature authorizes publish-merge

narrative.capability: have accepting a feature authorize the publish-merge node (or surface a manual-merge action) under the controlplane-issued GitHub App token
narrative.outcome: approved features land without me leaving the console, and the merge is gated by an explicit human approval

acceptanceCriteria.given: a feature I've accepted whose flow has a publish-merge node
acceptanceCriteria.then: the merge runs under the controlplane GitHub App token, the PR is merged, the audit entry records the approval as the authorizing event, and the kit lifecycle moves to accepted

stories.title: Submit a kit from the queue with the right context

narrative.capability: pick a handed-off spec kit from a queue (with provenance), choose a flow, configure the run (sandbox/real, overrides, iteration note), and submit
narrative.outcome: turning a spec into a job is a few clicks with the right kit, flow, and context

acceptanceCriteria.then: I see its contents (spec, wireframes, criteria) and the target product's available flows, with the suggested flow pre-selected
a job is created, linked to the kit version, and I get a link to watch it

stories.title: Browse the catalog and enable skills for the org

narrative.capability: browse the catalog of skills/agents/flows and enable skills for my org
narrative.outcome: I know what's available to wire into flows and submit against, and only enabled skills are usable

acceptanceCriteria.then: I see skills/agents/flows with descriptions and enabled/disabled state for my org

acceptanceCriteria.when: I enable it for my org
acceptanceCriteria.then: it becomes selectable in the Compose node picker; skillzkit remains the upstream source of truth

storyMaps.name: AgentX Control Plane v1
storyMaps.anchor: Operators turn product specs into safely-shipped, validated features via agent flows.

backbone.name: Set up a product
backbone.description: Register a product: name/productId, attach repos (GitHub App auth), configure + preview the default context source, activate.

backbone.name: Author & test flows
backbone.description: Author FlowDefs to cover job-request types (coverage matrix, node canvas, live validation, dry-run, versioning); test a flow in sandbox mode and iterate.

backbone.name: Browse & enable skills
backbone.description: Browse skills/agents/flows; enable skills for the org (skillzkit is upstream source of truth); pick into Compose nodes.

backbone.name: Spec a feature
backbone.description: Build a spec kit (one versioned object): research (citable), wireframes (linked to spec sections), feature spec (structured, regulated-data section), stories; embed generate-* flows; package with a completeness check.

backbone.name: Hand off a spec kit
backbone.description: Hand a packaged kit to the Outcome Engineer with a target product/flow and full provenance; track its lifecycle (handed-off → submitted → running → produced → accepted/rejected).

backbone.name: Submit a job
backbone.description: Pick a handed-off kit from the queue, choose a flow, configure the run (sandbox/real, overrides, iteration note), submit; the job links to the kit version.

backbone.name: Watch the run
backbone.description: Job flow graph with the current node highlighted, per-node status, cost/time; 'which node failed and why' summary; job actions (stop/retry/re-submit) that state their effects.

backbone.name: Inspect agent work
backbone.description: Live agent work: streaming logs, per-worktree diffs, agent state. Depth: a decision trace above the raw firehose; a manageable N-agent × M-worktree view.

backbone.name: Validate & decide
backbone.description: Produced feature side-by-side with its spec (criteria, wireframes) + diff; automated checks pre-populate a shared diff-linked acceptance-criteria checklist; decide accept / reject→flow (with corrections payload) / reject→Product, with an audit entry; accept → publish-merge under the GitHub App token.

backbone.name: Benchmark outcomes
backbone.description: Run agents+configs against benchmark suites; compare scores across runs. Not yet flow-mapped or storied — backbone placeholder, out of v1 scope.

slices.name: v1 — walking skeleton
slices.description: Smallest end-to-end pipeline: register a product → author+test a flow → spec a kit → hand off → submit a job → watch it → validate & merge. Thin per screen, complete across the whole loop. (Backbone step 8/Sessions covered by the existing basic streaming view; step 10/Benchmarks out of scope.)

slices.name: v1.1+ — depth (no new screens)
slices.description: Deepen the v1 screens: activation warnings, dry-run, flow versioning, decision-trace + multi-agent Sessions, intervention effect-clarity, cost/time visibility, research traceability, wireframe↔spec mapping, spec structure enforcement, kit packaging completeness, embedded generate-* flows, kit lifecycle tracking, automated-checks pre-population, corrections payload.
